entry.title: SAP Build - Actions (Spanish)
entry.content:
Han pasado más de dos años desde que comencé a usar SAP Build Apps (anteriormente conocido como AppGyver) y he visto la evolución de esta herramienta. Como cualquier otra herramienta, no es perfecta, pero tiene sus buenos momentos. En este blog, me gustaría hablar sobre la nueva función llamada Actions. Para ver este blog en ingles, haga clic aquí.

 

Actions (acciones) en el contexto de SAP Build Apps permiten a los desarrolladores de bajo código/no código (LCNC) encapsular APIs de forma sencilla y usarlas dentro de la lógica de SAP Build Apps. Todas las aplicaciones, independientemente de su tamaño, realizan llamadas a backend de una forma u otra; piense en la integración de sistemas SAP y no SAP.

Anteriormente, escribí sobre la carga de archivos a AWS mediante una función lambda y también sobre el consumo de una API REST directamente desde una aplicación LCNC (ver otros blogs al final del blog).

En este blog, quiero mostrar cómo encapsular la invocación de una llamada a la función AWS Lambda desde el punto de vista de las acciones y luego mostrar cómo usarla desde SAP Build Apps.

 

Asumiendo que ya ha configurado los requisitos previos en su entorno de SAP Build Apps (blog oficial de SAP aquí https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-trial..html😞

  1. Habilitar los servicios de identidad en la nube (Cloud identity services)
  2. Permitir la confianza (allow trust)
  3. Agregar colecciones de roles (role collections)
  4. Agregar usuarios a colecciones de roles

Los pasos anteriores le permitirán acceder a la herramienta SAP Build Apps para desarrollar aplicaciones LCNC. Una vez que su entorno esté configurado... debe crear un Destino en el cockpit de BTP y acceder a él desde allí. https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-cf-create-destination..html

  • Si su destino está creado y accesible, debe agregar las siguientes propiedades adicionales para asegurarse de que sea visible desde la herramienta SAP Build Apps https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/sap-systems:
  • Hacer disponible el destino desde SAP Build Apps
  • Permitir el uso del destino desde la sección "Actions" en SAP Build Apps Propiedad Valor

Property

value

sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled

true

BuildApps.Enabled

true

BuildApps.Type

rest / odata

 

 

    •  

Actions se crean desde un proyecto de acciones, como se describe en la documentación oficial: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/create-actions-project.

Ahora, existen diferentes formas de lograr la integración, tales como, consumir destinos de BTP, consumir funciones en la nube o consumir APIs OData de otros sistemas SAP (integración directa con SuccessFactors, Concur, CAP, RAP). En mi caso, voy a consumir una API REST envuelta como una Acción en mi aplicación de Build Apps.

Una vez en SAP Build Apps, en la navegación de la izquierda, hay un Lobby, Connectors y Control Tower.

  • Lobby es donde puede crear sus aplicaciones SAP Build Apps (front y back end).
  • Connectors es donde puede crear proyectos de acciones basados en fuentes de API [RAP, CAP, destinos BTP, SuccessFactors, Concur].

 

Cuando abre el proyecto de Acción que creó, puede ver las acciones en su proyecto. Al seleccionarla, cada acción tiene un endpoint, una sección de Input, una sección de Output y una Test (autoexplicativo).

 

La pestaña Test es mi favorita porque puede verificar que el destino sea alcanzable y que haya flujo de datos en la herramienta SAP Build Apps (antes de hacer esto, también puede verificar su servicio desde POSTMAN o incluso que la conexión con los Destinos funcione en su cockpit de BTP).

Una vez en la pestaña, puede seleccionar la opción Manual (escribirá una URL, tipo de autenticación o puede seleccionar un destino si configuró las propiedades adicionales en su sección de Destinos del cockpit de BTP como se mostró arriba). Además, hay una sección para proporcionar sus valores de entrada en caso de que deba pasar parámetros de entrada (query string, header, path). Si todo está configurado correctamente y obtiene una respuesta exitosa (después de hacer clic en el botón de Test), podrá analizar la respuesta haciendo clic en el enlace "Ver API" en la parte inferior derecha de la pantalla (está un poco oculto, pero está allí).

Se abrirá una sección de Vista previa de prueba y podrá ver la respuesta de su Acción. Si llega aquí y puede ver una respuesta, ¡felicitaciones! Está en un buen lugar. Ahora no necesita conocer el esquema exacto de su salida. Una vez que tenga una respuesta exitosa, puede asignarla para que se pre-poblen los datos haciendo clic en el botón "Generar Output" (esta característica merece 5 estrellas en mi opinión). Ahora puede consumir datos, pasar parámetros y obtener una respuesta, todo desde SAP Build Apps.

 

Si crea una nueva Acción o realiza cambios en una acción existente, asegúrese de hacer clic en el botón "Release" y luego hacer Publish para que se refleje en SAP Build Apps.

El siguiente paso y nueva característica es el flujo de lógica dentro de SAP Build Apps en sí. No entraré en detalles sobre cómo crear una aplicación, ya que mencioné varios blogs a continuación que puede consultar para comenzar, o mejor aún, visite el grupo Builders en #SAPCommunity para ver qué sucede en ese grupo y conectarse con otros Constructores SAP como yo.

En la imagen a continuación, abrí SAP Build Apps y ya había agregado algunos componentes al lienzo. Sabía que quería invocar la función AWS Lambda desde el clic de un botón después de leer una entrada de un usuario.

  1. El campo de Input establece el valor en una Variable de Página
  2. El evento de clic del botón activa el flujo a continuación:
  1. Condición para verificar que hay un valor no vacío; de lo contrario, mostrar una Alerta.
  2. La Trigger Action llama a la Acción que además llama al destino y, por lo tanto, a la función Lambda 😊
  3. Tras la respuesta de la Acción, muestro un mensaje de Toast con la respuesta (JSON).

 

 

Existen otros nuevos flujos de lógica que no menciono en este blog. Los invito a investigarlos. Quería ver cómo funcionaba esta nueva característica y la facilidad (o dificultad) para lograr esta integración entre SAP Build Apps, BTP - Destinations y AWS Lambda.

Al concluir este blog, realmente creo que este ejercicio fue más fácil de lo que pensé.

  1. Me ahorró tiempo debido al enfoque LCNC.
  2. Me ahorró en el proceso de integración, evitando tener que saber cómo estructurarlo en un entorno de codificación profesional.
  3. Me ahorró el dolor de cabeza de crear types/clases aprovechando la estructura de Generar Output.
  4. Me ahorró tiempo al validar la integración y asegurarme de que los datos fluyeran.

Cosas que no tuvieron sentido para mí:

  • Logo de SAP en la esquina superior derecha: ¿es esta la única forma de volver a la página de inicio (Lobby, Connectors)?
  • Acciones → versiones: ¿cuántas podemos mantener? ¿Por qué necesitaríamos muchas versiones?
  • Publicar una acción debería actualizar automáticamente la versión en SAP Build Apps; sin embargo, cuando navegué a la sección de Integraciones de la aplicación en sí, tuve que hacer clic en la burbuja que me mostraba que se había actualizado.

Sugerencia: Cuando descargue las aplicaciones de vista previa de SAP Build Apps, asegúrese de seleccionar la región correcta antes de generar un PIN; de lo contrario, obtendrá mensajes de error de pin incorrectos.

Otros blogs que escribí en el anteriormente:

  1. "To code or not to code, that is the question": https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/appgyver-to-code-or-not-to-code-that-is-the-question/ba-p/13544885
  2. "Master list detail" - https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/appgyver-master-detail-scenario/ba-p/13526977
  3. "Uploading a file to AWS (via calling lambda function)" - https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/build-bros-handling-file-upload-via-http-request/ba-p/13551643
  4. "Funny app for kids" - https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/dadiambored-no-code-challenge/ba-p/13530701

Aquí está la misma aplicación desde mi dispositivo móvil.

 

  Después de llamar la acción à  

 

Gracias por su tiempo leyendo este blog. Por favor, hágame saber si tiene alguna pregunta, comentario o sugerencia.

¡Feliz desarrollo de bajo/no código!

SG

entry.title: SAP is a Visionary in 2024 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Enterprise Low-Code Application Platforms
entry.content:
Today, I am very proud to share the great news: Gartner has recognized SAP as a Visionary in its 2024 Magic Quadrant for Enterprise Low-Code Application Platforms (LCAP).

Let me share with you how we got here, what we believe makes us a Visionary in low-code application development with SAP Build and how you can get started with SAP’s one-stop-shop for extensibility and automations.

SAP Build – the low-code platform for SAP customers

We have designed SAP Build as the best software suite for SAP customers that want to efficiently extend their SAP solutions: SAP Build fosters fusion development allowing active collaboration between business users and professional developers, it brings Business AI with Joule copilot to boost development productivity, prebuilt LoB solutions with context-based recommendations, and deep integration with business applications.

A bit of history

Two years ago, at SAP TechEd 2022, we announced the launch of a powerful new offering to drive the next wave of business transformation by unleashing the expertise of those who know best in the lines of business.
SAP Build has been designed to put SAP’s world-class enterprise technology in the hands of multidisciplinary teams in business and IT, giving them direct, secure access to the end-to-end processes, data and context they need to make smarter decisions and drive innovation quickly. SAP Build allows business experts and, in the SAP world “Key Users”, to fruitfully collaborate with IT and developers to iterate and co-create new innovations.

Last year, at SAP TechEd 2023, we announced SAP Build Code, which went generally available in March this year. Tailored for SAP development, SAP Build Code is a comprehensive, turn-key solution for coding, testing, integrations, and application lifecycle management. Developers can leverage Joule to efficiently generate application logic, data models, and test scripts. SAP Build Code enhances the entire development process, from design time to runtime, significantly boosting developers’ productivity.

And recently at SAP TechEd 2024 we achieved another milestone by adding ABAP Environments to SAP Build: with this, SAP Build is THE extensibility solution for any SAP customer: you can now accelerate your ERP extension development and modernization, foster innovation, and automate processes, all in a single, comprehensive suite of AI-driven tools for extension development.

SAP Build is now the fastest growing area in SAP Business Technology Platform: we have doubled our revenue over the last year, increasing by more than 70% the active customer base. And we have had more than 200,000 active learners.

What makes SAP a visionary with SAP Build?

From my personal point of view, here are the key factors that make SAP Build the best development platform for SAP customers:

Fusion Team Development with one SAP Build offering

SAP is promoting the concept of fusion team development. Instead of converting pro-developers s to low-code developers (or vice versa), it combines its traditional developer stack with low-code SAP Build products. This multipronged development strategy resonates with enterprise developers and IT leaders who want the flexibility to use a range of approaches to build custom applications.

Fusion development is an enabler to be quicker with extending applications to your needs. What’s more, it is the foundation of allowing multidisciplinary teams to create connected solutions that fit the business’ needs, adds more people to the development and innovation equation which allows a much faster time to market, more fine-grained solutions shaped to the experts’ needs, and brings diversity into the innovation process.

Among our key innovations in Fusion Development:

  • Easy Project Discovery: Find, share and reuse application components with a centralized project lobby
  • Seamless Teamwork: Multiple developers can work simultaneously with version control and synchronized changes
  • Low-Code with Pro-Code Power: Business users can easily integrate pre-built solutions created by developers
  • Intuitive Iteration: Modify application, process & workspace designs and logic with drag-and drop ease, fostering collaboration between business and IT

Integrated Business AI capabilities

Assistance is what we expect from AI, to automate tedious tasks or to become faster with our assignments. Leveraging AI to speed up development productivity includes content recommendation sensitive to a certain context you find yourself in, allows you to naturally describe a process you’d like to create or even a data model to be created so you can focus on the creative parts of the app dev cycle, for instance, the UI creation. For that, SAP has integrated in SAP Build advanced AI capabilities that are grounded in business data: SAP Business AI understands business lingo and context. In addition, we recently launched at SAP TechEd the Joule Studio in SAP Build that will help customers and partners to build extensibility scenarios for Joule with custom skills and AI agents. So, Generative AI and Joule copilot are infused everywhere in SAP Build:

  • Application Development: Creation of business applications and automations
  • Process Automation: Process generation, process monitoring, document extraction and pre-built solution recommendations
  • Integration: Integration flow generation, pre-built integration recommendations
  • Joule Studio in SAP Build: Creating custom AI agents and skills for Joule copilot

Prebuilt LoB solutions

Content that allows developers to start with a ready-made template for their development is key for speeding up the creation process. Therefore, we are and have been investing heavily into content creation, be it ready-made APIs, process content packages, or application templates. Anything that is needed from a developer’s perspective to ease their work and make them faster in their creation process is available at hand in SAP Build available through the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Both SAP and partners contribute, maintain and update pre-built content. We have now more than 500 content packages with 120+ in Finance, 115+ in Supply Chain, 40+ in Human Resources and our recent innovations in this area include, but are not limited to:

  • Finance: Capital expenditure application, automation of accrual posting and more
  • Supply Chain: Process approval for SAP IBP, Purchase requisition approval for Ariba and more
  • Human Resources: Translate job postings, position management action and more

Deep integration with business apps

SAP is unifying and standardizing its application runtimes and developer tools for numerous developer personas and use cases. This new product composition makes it easier for customers to consume SAP Build and establish partnership links. It also provides the SAP Store and SAP Business Accelerator Hub offering prebuilt reusable components that accelerate application delivery.

Recently, we have delivered:

  • Navigation from the business application UI with contextualized Extensibility Wizard to initiate an SAP Build project: available already in in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition, and soon in other SAP applications, the Extensibility Wizard is an embedded experience allowing key users to extend business applications – both on stack and side by side on SAP BTP – directly out of their business application without losing context.

For more details on how to set up the Extensibility Wizard and what extensions options are available, please read this blog post: “SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition” and watch this Devtoberfest session by @Sesh_Sreenivas and @Karthik-Thiru.

  • Extensive library of connectors, integration flows and business events for SAP and non-SAP applications in SAP Business Accelerator Hub:

Learn more and get started with SAP Build!

To learn how SAP Build can optimize your finance, procurement, human resources, and more take the next step with the following resources:

  • Visit the SAP Build product page
  • If you are using S/4HANA, visit also our SAP Build for SAP S/4HANA page
  • Discover pre-built content packages on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub
  • Explore the basic trial and start your learning journey
  • Activate your entitlements if you’re an existing RISE and GROW with SAP customer

Use SAP Build now, not only to easily create applications, but also to automate processes and to design digital workspaces with low code and generative AI!

 

 

GARTNER is a registered trademark and service mark of Gartner and Magic Quadrant is a registered trademark of Gartner, Inc. and/or its affiliates in the U.S. and internationally and are used herein with permission. All rights reserved.

This graphic was published by Gartner, Inc. as part of a larger research document and should be evaluated in the context of the entire document. The Gartner document is available upon request from SAP.

Gartner does not endorse any vendor, product or service depicted in its research publications, and does not advise technology users to select only those vendors with the highest ratings or other designation. Gartner research publications consist of the opinions of Gartner’s research organization and should not be construed as statements of fact. Gartner disclaims all warranties, expressed or implied, with respect to this research, including any warranties of merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose.

entry.title: Steer the future direction of SAP Build at the SAP BTP Usability Event
entry.content:
Do you work with SAP Build products like Workzone, CDS-Modeller, Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation, UI5? Would you like to help steer the future direction of these products?
Then our exclusive 2-day SAP BTP Usability Event is for you!
(Last remaining seats still available. Book yours by November 15th)

Join us on November 25th & 26th, 2024 at our headquarters in Walldorf, where you can:

  • Gain early insights into SAP's product vision, ideas, and research projects
  • Share ideas directly with the development teams and influence product development
  • Participate in the development of products that better meet your needs and help your business
  • Provide valuable input on new features directly to the engineering teams during 1:1 usability testing sessions
  • Access expert talks and networking opportunities

What next? Please write me a DM (direct message) before November 15th to secure your place, and I can send you all the details around the registration, full agenda, accommodation etc.

Looking forward to seeing you there! #btpusabilityevent #sapbuild #usertesting

entry.title: Discover TechEd “Live” at SAPinsider EMEA 2024
entry.content:
Following hot on the heels of the hugely successful SAP TechEd Virtual event last month, attention now turns to a number of live events that will feature TechEd content and speakers, starting at the ASUG Tech Connect event in West Palm Beach, the first of SAP’s “TechEd on Tour” locations, as well as featuring in sessions at the SAPinsider EMEA “TechInsider” event in Copenhagen. Both events run from 12- 14 November in these wonderful, if quite different, locations.

I’ve been deeply involved in the planning for the SAPinsider event again, and for this reason alone will focus my blog on that event, though I am hopeful that both events will deliver a rich experience of TechEd content for attendees, and allow them to delve deeply into SAP technologies, particularly relating to SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and Artificial Intelligence (AI).

At SAPinsider Copenhagen we’re going the extra mile, in trying to deliver a TechEd-style experience for attendees, who I’m happy to say will not only find an agenda littered with TechEd sessions, delivered by many of our stellar TechEd speakers, but also they’ll be able to participate in multiple hands-on sessions as well as having an opportunity to continue their learning experience by networking with SAP’s Developer Advocates who will guide and support attendees as they work through a number of the tutorials available at the Developer Garage on the show floor – which we are pleased will make its very first appearance at this SAPinsider event.

With all this on offer I’m excited for what’s in store in Copenhagen next week – and I hope that you are too!

Plan Ahead – And Set Your Agenda Now!

For those of you registered for the event, and in a manner which is not unlike visiting a Disney theme park, my suggestion for you to get the most out of an SAPinsider event is to plan ahead – not so much to beat the queues as you might at the theme park, but more so to make sure that you see all you want to see, and achieve what you set out to do. And the best way to do this is to prepare your agenda for the event before you go.

The first step is of course to get the event app, “SAPinsider Events”, available on the App Store and Google Play, and once you’ve installed that you’re good to go in terms of adding sessions to your personal agenda. Now for the fun bit – what sessions to see? Perhaps I might help with some ideas.

I’ll assume that everyone will plan to join the day 1 and day 2 keynotes. I’m looking forward to both, the motivational first day keynote from Hein Wagner, starting at 9.00am on Tuesday 12th November, followed the next day by the AI inspired second day keynote, featuring Martin Böhm of Ottobock and Jonathan Von Rüden of SAP, and which starts at the same time of day on the 13th November.

But after the keynotes, what next? Here are my personal 6 TechEd inspired picks for Copenhagen:

  1. Enhancing SAP Development Processes with SAP Build and ABAP Cloud Interoperability to Elevate Your Extensions, 12-Nov-24, 10:30 - 11:15
  2. Hands-On Lab: Develop Applications and Processes with SAP Build and Joule, 12-Nov-24, 15:30 - 17:30
  3. Mastering SAP Business AI for Enhanced Performance and Innovation, 13-Nov-24, 10:30 - 11:15
  4. SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere Solutions: Integration, 13-Nov-24, 14:15 - 15:00
  5. Clean Core Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, 13-Nov-24, 16:45 - 17:30
  6. SAP BTP Strategy and Highlights, 14-Nov-24, 09:00 - 09:45

Of course, this is just the tip of the iceberg, as there are many more sessions available for you to select from the agenda, and among which you’ll find more TechEd content, as well as content from other speakers from SAP and our business partners, in addition to many customer case stories too. And in a shameless plug too, you may also note my session “Navigating Your Path to Success with SAP BTP” which I shall deliver alongside Nis Boy Naeve on 13 November at 11:30am. Please join us if you can! Take a look at the SAPinsider event website sessions list, or the event app that you’ve already downloaded to see more.

Your Continued Learning Experience

If you’d like to continue your own personal learning journey after the event, then why not explore other learning opportunities from SAP? For example, learn how to build software applications, side-by-side extensions, and integrations to and from cloud applications by exploring SAP’s free learning content on SAP BTP. It is made for both integration designers and extension developers from all levels of expertise and will help you stay up to date with the latest SAP BTP innovations. And check out even more role-based learning resources and opportunities to get certified in one place on SAP Learning site.

Or if you’re interested in TechEd sessions, but can’t join the events in West Palm Beach or Copenhagen, then perhaps another TechEd on Tour location, in Australia, Germany or the UK would work better for you?

For those of you travelling to Copenhagen next week, I do hope that you find SAPinsider EMEA 2024 to be a truly informative and stimulating experience. And, of course, if you visit the show floor then look me up at the SAP exhibit booth, and I’ll be happy to have a chat with you about SAP BTP, the conference or just about the amazing city of Copenhagen!

My best wishes for safe travels, and I hope that you enjoy the event!

Chris Grundy

Product Marketing, SAP BTP

entry.title: 🎄 Get into the holiday spirit with our SAP Build & AGILITA Workshop! ✨
entry.content:
Are you an SAP customer interested in SAP S/4HANA Extensibility or SAP Business AI use cases?

If yes, plan a visit to Berlin end of this month, and get into the holiday spirit with our very special SAP Build & AGILITA Workshop!

We are happy to offer you:

  • Product deep dives: Jumpstart your SAP Build product knowledge with a hands-on exercise, including a gen-AI scenario connected to SAP S/4HANA for application development.
  • Discussions with experts: Gain valuable insights about our portfolio and strategy, while sharing your questions with our technical experts.
  • Christmas Market Fun: Explore SAP Build solutions in an authentic Christmas Market, where you can get to know the products and teams, along with some warm glühwein and waffles!

 🎄 This isn’t your everyday workshop, as we’ve planned a unique experience with a festive holiday theme to go along with your introduction to SAP Build! ✨
 

DATE

📅  November 28 - 11:00am - 7:00pm CET

LOCATION

📍 Berlin

WHO SHOULD ATTEND

Developers, team leads, and also business users interested in extending core processes with SAP Build.

AGENDA

Part 1 - SAP Berlin office

  • 11:00am - Lunch & networking
  • 11:30am - Opening keynotes + Q&A
  • 12:15pm - Product deep dives + Q&A
  • 1:00pm - Hands-on exercise
  • 4:00pm - Transportation to AGILITA office (provided by SAP)

Part 2 - AGILITA office

  • 4:30pm - Christmas Market
  • 7:00pm - Closing remarks

JOIN US!

If you’re interested, please register here and we will get back to you in a few hours to confirm your participation 👉

 

On a personal note, I'm very excited to be part of this special event, which will be a unique opportunity to engage with SAP customers... and also to catch up with dear colleagues I haven't seen in person for a long time. I'm also looking forward to meeting people from our valued partner AGILITA, whom I interviewed a year and a half ago.

So, see you in Berlin on 28 November?

entry.title: Which S/4HANA Extensibility Options should I use as a SAP customer?
entry.content:
Get more value from S/4HANA with SAP Extensibility options, part 2

In my first article of this series, I explained why extensibility options are crucial for S/4HANA customers. In the last article, the third of this series, I will focus on existing and new SAP partners who wish to connect their cloud offerings to S/4HANA customers or migrate their existing add-on to ABAP clean-code code. In this article, I will explain in more depth from a customer's point of view which Extensibility Options fit the best different use cases for an SAP S/4HANA Cloud system.

As explained in my previous article, software vendors like SAP consistently deliver best-practice solutions that should fit multiple customers. This means there will always be a gap between the requirements for your business processes and the software solution delivered to support them. There are several ways to close this gap. You can manage the gap by supporting it with manual procedures, but you can also try to solve it by extending your software solution with other solutions and custom development. In this article, I will focus on the following topics:

  1. Adopt your system by customizing your business processes
  2. Add ready-made solutions like SAP cloud products, SAP third-party add-ons, and SAP cloud partner solutions
  3. Personalize the UI using S/4HANA Personalization Options
  4. Build your own extensions using S/4HANA Extensibility Options

Customize your business processes.

The best and cheapest option to solve the gap is to customize your business process to fit into SAP's best practice processes. By using the predefined templates and configuration options of S/4HANA, you can tailor your business processes to your needs. For S/4HANA public cloud, there is a specific cloud application called SAP Central Business Configuration, which enables you to scope, configure, and implement end-to-end business processes for your cloud solution from a central place. After setting up your S/4HANA Cloud public edition, you can use the Implementation Activities app for further configurations, such as in SAP ECC and other S/4HANA editions, using transaction SPRO.

Figure 1 - SAP Central Business Configuration for S/4HANA Cloud Public Editions

 

 

SAP cloud products, SAP third-party add-ons, and SAP cloud partner solutions

When a gap remains between your business processes and the customizing, you should consider SAP cloud products, SAP S/4HANA on-stack third-party add-ons, or side-by-side SAP cloud partner solutions in the SAP store.

As mentioned in my previous article, SAP simplified the supported processes in the S/4HANA Cloud public edition. It moved functionality to other SAP Cloud products, providing even more functionality in their domain. These solutions can provide even more functionality than previously available in SAP ECC. In the last couple of years, SAP has invested a lot in integrating these SAP Cloud products with S/4HANA and has now positioned the complete integrated portfolio as Cloud ERP. However, with the simplification of S/4HANA, customers can also choose products from an SAP competitor to solve the missing functionality in S/4HANA for some domains. This results in some challenges that I will discuss later in this article.

Another way to solve the gap between business processes and SAP software is to install a third-party SAP add-on to the S/4HANA system or connect your system to a SAP side-by-side cloud partner solution. In the past, add-ons were often considered, and for SAP ECC, many add-ons were available. But for S/4HANA Cloud editions, this isn’t the case yet. SAP has previously asked its partners to build their solutions outside S/4HANA, in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), and provide this as an SAP cloud partner solution. SAP even set up a partner program to run these solutions as a SAAS offering on BTP, which makes it very easy for S/4HANA customers to consume these partner solutions. The possibility of closing the gap by providing SAAS offerings on BTP by a SAP cloud partner is a crucial aspect of SAP’s strategy to extend its footprint into the market. And from that aspect, I expect many more SAP cloud partner solutions soon.

However, besides the SAAS offering, I expect many more S/4HANA on-stack add-on products soon. SAP has launched the embedded ABAP Cloud stack of S/4HANA Cloud public edition 2023. This allows smaller SAP partners to deliver their add-ons based on ABAP Cloud to customers via a controlled git-based multi-off delivery. A SAP certification for this addon is unnecessary because the code follows the SAP clean core paradigm, which will be checked when you deploy your code. This removes the barrier for many SAP partners, consulting companies, and independent consultants to build and provide add-ons to their customers.

S/4HANA Personalization Options

In some cases, the gap is small and depends on the user of the S/4HANA system. You can influence how users work with the system by providing the proper business role. Authorizations and using default values in the Fiori Launchpad already allow you to influence and limit user entry, but you can go further.

S/4HANA Uis, based on Fiori Elements, can be configured to present data in a personalized way. You can add and remove filters and their values, change how the data is displayed, and store and share this personalized look and feel.

S/4HANA Key User Extensibility – UI adaption

Sometimes, these personalization capabilities aren’t enough, and you want to adapt the UI and change the look and feel of the applications to your needs. You can change or hide titles, buttons, and label names and reorder and hide fields and sections. If the S/4HANA app supports this option and you have the proper authorization, you will find the UI Adapt button in the menu of your Fiori Launchpad profile.

UI adaptation, default values, and personalization are strong capabilities of S/4HANA Cloud that can improve user experiences and reduce users' knowledge gaps.

S/4HANA Extensibility Options

Figure 2 - Extensibility options

For some business processes, more than all of the options above are needed, and the only solution is to use the other S/4HANA Extensibility Options. My previous article mentioned that SAP provides eight possible S/4HANA Extensibility Options, as seen in the picture above. Before I elaborate on them, I want to address two topics that are relevant to understanding the S/4HANA Extensibility options:

  1. On-stack extensions versus Side-by-Side extensions
  2. Low-code versus Pro-Code

On-stack extensions versus Side-by-Side extensions

On-stack extensions are S/4HANA extensibility options embedded in the S/4HANA system. All capabilities, authorizations, support, and maintenance are directly dependent on the availability of your S/4HANA system, and these extensibility options are included in the S/4HANA licenses. Because these extensibility options run on the same instance as the S/4HANA system, they are tightly coupled. This allows for adopting extensions to standard S/4HANA processes and handling big datasets with excellent performance.

Side-by-side extensions are S/4HANA Extensibility Options, which run on their own instances in the SAP BTP Cloud environment. These extensions are loosely coupled with S/4HANA. These solutions will exchange data with S/4HANA through APIs and events. These extensibility options are primarily suitable when:

  • The on-stack extension significantly impacts the available S/4HANA runtime resources;
  • Casual or external users must participate. Still, you don’t want to give them direct or full access to S/4HANA;
  • you need an extension, which, besides S/4HANA, also needs data from other cloud resources;
  • you need solution extensions for multiple S/4HANA systems.

Low-code versus Pro-Code

The S/4HANA extensibility options are split into low-code and pro-code options. The pro-code option is the most flexible and powerful option for realizing your extensions, but you also need a professional SAP developer to build the extension. In many cases, you can realize the extensions by just adopting some slight modifications using applications that do this through minimal coding, supported mainly by a visual approach. In this case, you don’t need a professional SAP developer in the IT department; you can let business users with some coding knowledge build the extension. These types of business users who are using low-code applications are also known as citizen developers.

S/4HANA Key User Extensibility Option.

Figure 3 - Key User Extensibility Option

 The S/4HANA Key User Extensibility Option is a low-code extensibility option in the S/4HANA system. It provides a set of Fiori apps to manage the most common changes needed to User Interfaces (UIs) and business processes.

Extend standard SAP UIs, reports, and APIs with custom fields.

With Custom Fields, you can create new custom fields and extend existing SAP data structures with calculated fields. This allows you to customize applications and adapt their UIs, reports, email templates, form templates, and APIs.

This custom fields capability allows storing, calculating, and using additional fields in your S/4HANA applications. It will follow SAP's Clean Core strategy and is stable for upgrades and future-proof. With this option, you don’t need to improperly use existing SAP fields of SAP applications, which has happened a lot in the past in SAP ECC.

Influence the behavior of a S/4HANA application.

With Custom Logic, you can influence the behavior of standard S/4HANA applications. It will replace all the options we had in the past in SAP ECC, such as user exists, modifications, implicit enhancements, and enhancement spots. SAP has released predefined extension points in its standard SAP code lines, which can be influenced by custom code. Predefined extension points are available as business add-ins (BAdIs), and you can add the logic you need with this app. You can use a limited set of statements to influence the behavior of standard SAP business objects, interact with your custom business objects in the system, and even call services outside the S/4HANA system to create, update, or retrieve data from an external resource. To call services outside S/4HANA, you can use the Custom Communication Scenario app to configure the connectivity.

Implementing released BAdIs is the only future-proof solution in SAP’s Clean Core strategy that can influence the standard logic.

Building simple Custom Business Objects, transactions, and APIs.

With the Custom Business Object app, a citizen developer can develop small custom S/4HANA applications and backend services for new developments in S/4HANA and replace current custom-built applications in SAP ECC. The app allows you to create a custom data model with nodes and fields, add limited business logic such as determinations and validations, and generate the custom business object, the UI, and the backend service. When artifacts like fields, structures, value help, and methods are needed for multiple Custom Business Objects, you can use the Customer Reusable Elements app to build these artifacts.

The custom business object can be used in the custom logic of a standard SAP application. It can also be accessed as a Fiori application by S/HANA users when the object is added as an extension to an existing catalog using the app Custom Catalog Extensions. The custom business object can also be used by applications outside S/4HANA when the object's backend service is generated and made publicly available after you process it with the app Custom Communication Scenario.

Make external web resources available.

The Custom Tile app allows you to create Fiori tiles in your Fiori Launchpad to access external web applications. Since the user must already have access rights to external web applications, this solution is best for other SAP cloud applications, publicly accessible internet applications, and custom-built and third-party applications running on SAP BTP, which shares the same identity provider as S/4HANA.

Assemble analytical reports and custom data sources.

In the clean core strategy of S/4HANA, you can’t access SAP tables directly anymore, but you should access your data through so-called CDS views. The CDS Views can be a projection layer on top of these tables but can also read data from other CDS views. The CDS views serve as a source for transactional and analytical apps, value-helps, APIs, and data extraction. SAP uses these CDS views to migrate its existing data model slightly to a new Virtual Data Model. The Virtual Data Model of SAP has many CDS views, but only the released CDS views can be used by SAP partners and customers.

The Custom CDS View app allows customers to assemble custom CDS views for analytical and read-only external APIs. They are based on released CDS views and other custom content, such as custom business objects and other custom CDS views. With the app, you can create a CDS View that selects fields from multiple data sources and adds new calculated fields. You can refine these chosen fields' properties, add filters to refine the result set and add and maintain parameters for the usage within your view.

The Custom CDS View app also allows users to create CDS Views with analytical capabilities, the so-called Analytical CDS View. These views can be used in S/4HANA analytical tooling, the S/4HANA query builder app Custom Analytical Queries, S/4HANA KPI Designer, and APF Configuration Modeling.

Reports and overview page.

In SAP's Clean Core strategy, SAP Fiori is the only supported UI for custom-built applications in S/4HANA. This means that all UI capabilities of SAP ECC, such as ABAP reports, ABAP dynpro, BSP, and WebDynpro, are obsolete and should be replaced.

However, this is easy for Custom Business Objects or custom CDS views. When these artifacts are exposed as S/4HANA external APIs, these APIs can be used as a source for the Fiori Elements tooling. With this tooling, a Fiori List Reports, Fiori Analytical List Page, and Fiori Overview Page can be generated in a few steps. A frontend developer can take this as a base, add the needed UI capabilities to the generated application, and deploy it to the S/4HANA system as a Custom Fiori App.

Real-time analytic dashboards.

When the analytical capabilities in S/4HANA and Fiori Elements don’t fit your business needs, you can always use a side-by-side tool like SAP Analytics Cloud. This is appropriate when using S/4HANA and other data resources to analyze and predict business outcomes and want to use the outcomes for business intelligence and enterprise planning.

S/4HANA supports this Extensibility Option by providing a live data connection between S/4HANA  and SAP Analytics Cloud based on SAP’s optimized InA protocol. This allows SAP Analytics Cloud to query S/4HANA's data sources in real time and react directly to changes.

The SAP Analytics Cloud Extensibility Option allows real-time dashboarding of business processes. The live data connection, machine learning, and artificial intelligence capabilities of SAP Analytics Cloud will also take the S/4HANA-supported business process to the next level, which isn’t possible with SAP ECC.

Process automation and interaction with occasional SAP users.

In most companies, a business process starts with collecting, checking, and approving information before it is entered into an ERP system. This process is usually manual and supported by unstructured data tools such as email, spreadsheets, documents, and notes.

The market recognized this and developed more structured, low-code solutions to support these processes. SAP entered this market, too, with SAP Build, a citizen development tool for building workflows and simple UIs to collect, check, and approve data for users who do not need access to a S/4HANA system.

When SAP examined these processes, it saw another significant improvement opportunity. Most processes had repeated steps, such as collecting and interpreting data from known sources and manually entering data into applications and unstructured tools, which robotic engines and artificial intelligence can easily automate without interaction with a user. This process automation capability was also added to the SAP Build portfolio.

Figure 4 - SAP Build Process Automation

 

SAP positioned SAP Build Process Automation as a side-by-side extension possibility for additional customer-specific processes and automation on top of S/4HANA. It will also replace the customer ABAP workflows we know from SAP ECC, which is no longer possible in S/4HANA.

Integrate your business processes in the cloud.

Many companies use software products other than SAP to automate their business processes. They want to work more closely with their business partners and can gain value by outsourcing part of their business processes to them. Companies also want to automate data exchange between ERP and their manufacturing systems.

ERP systems have exchanged data with each other almost from the beginning. In the early days, users had to make manual entries, but nowadays, they can use files and spreadsheets or even connect systems with APIs using EDI or internet-based networks. The data model should be aligned, and integration products should be introduced to map the content and API structures of the connected systems.

Integration becomes even more critical with the simplification of S/4HANA and the move of business functionality to other Cloud products. Traditional integration, as we know from SAP ECC, is not enough. By moving functionality to other cloud products, S/4HANA also loses the tight coupling between the business processes we had in SAP ECC. APIs alone are not enough to solve this issue. S/4HANA needs to provide notification events to connected systems when situations in the supported business processes are changed. The traditional integration architecture needs to shift to an event-driven architecture, precisely what SAP offers with its Cloud Integration Suite and S/4HANA Cloud events and APIs.

Figure 5 - SAP Cloud Integration Suite

Cloud Integration Suite offers traditional EAI/B2B integration options, the capability to connect to non-SAP products using open connectors, and SAP Graph, a fully event-driven unified data model on top of SAP’s Cloud products.

Building your enterprise-grade cloud applications.

When do you need a professional developer to build your custom application? As soon as you cannot solve the gap in our S/4HANA business process by connecting other software products, building process automation workflows and UIs, or modifying standard SAP screens and logic with key user extensibility options, you need a professional developer.

Of course, you can build this application on any platform in any language. However, when data from SAP Cloud products is involved, creating applications with SAP tooling is the best solution. From the perspective of S/4HANA, you can develop the application on-stack or side-by-side.

On-stack is the best solution if you need tight integration with your S/4HANA data or users. In this case, you build your application directly with ABAP Cloud on the embedded ABAP environment, and you can use all of S/4HANA's capabilities. This is also known as SAP S/4HANA Cloud Developer Extensibility.

On the other hand, when you need an application loosely coupled from S/4HANA and mostly runs standalone or heavenly, depending on other SAP cloud products, the side-by-side extension SAP Build Code on BTP is the best solution. With SAP Build Code, a professional developer can build enterprise-grade Java and JavaScript cloud applications fast and with high quality, supported by AI-based code generation.

SAP BTP also offers an ABAP environment for developing side-by-side applications. When you upgrade to the S/4HANA cloud, this environment can be used to build new, future-proof, clean-core ABAP applications while still running SAP ECC. After the upgrade, these applications can later be moved as SAP S/4HANA Cloud Developer Extensibility of the embedded ABAP environment of your S/4HANA. However, you can also move your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Developer Extensibility applications to the SAP BTP ABAP Cloud environment when the applications significantly impact the runtime resources on the performance or cost of your S/4HANA system.

As I will explain in my next article, the SAP BTP ABAP environment can also be a good option for SAP solution partners to provide additional S/4HANA functionality in a software-as-a-service model.

Conclusion.

SAP supports many different S/4HANA Extensibility Options described in this article to help customers solve gaps in supporting their business processes with S/4HANA. SAP analyzes customers' needs by examining SAP ECC and translating this into options, considering their clean-core strategy for S/4HANA. This allows customers to adjust their business processes and offers many opportunities for SAP solution partners and cloud vendors to add value to the SAP ecosystem.

This article was initially posted on the linked-in profile of Robert Eijpe

entry.title: SAP Build Apps – REST API Integration - Customize CSRF Token Fetching URL
entry.content:
SAP Build Apps – REST API Integration - Customize CSRF Token Fetching URL

In this blog, I will discuss how to customize where to fetch the CSRF token for SAP Build Apps classic REST API integration. In my previous blog  SAP Build Apps - OData Integration – Customize CSRF Token Fetching URL we already discussed how to customize where to fetch the CSRF token for OData integration. But if your app is still using classic REST API integration, you may follow the steps below to customize where to fetch the CSRF token.

For example, if your BTP destination ‘MyRESTDest’ setting is as shown below

 

#
#Thu Sep 05 07:22:58 UTC 2024
Type=HTTP
BuildApps.RequiresCsrf=true
Name=MyRESTDest
URL=https\://mycompany.com/REST/myrestsvc
ProxyType=Internet
AppgyverEnabled=true

 

You want to customize your CSRF token fetch to https://mycompany.com/REST/myrestsvc/path/svc1

You need to follow steps as below

1. Drag the HTTP Request flow function to the logic canvas to fetch the CSRF token. It can be triggered by the Page Mounted event or any other event that occurs before you perform any HTTP POST, PUT, PATCH or DELETE request to your REST API integration.

 

2. Set the following inputs:

  • URL: destinations/MyRESTDest/path/srv1
  • HTTP method: GET
  • Headers: { value: Fetch, header: x-csrf-token }

Details are shown in the screenshot below:

 

3. Assign the output outputs["HTTP request"].resHeaders["x-csrf-token"] to an app variable, e.g., CSRFToken.

Details are shown in the screenshot below:

4. Add an additional input to your REST API integration, for example, csrfToken with the field type set to Text.

Details are shown in the screenshot below:

5. Assign the additional input csrfToken to a new common request header named x-csrf-token with the formula query.additionalInputs.csrfToken.

Details are shown in the screenshots below:

6. At the flow function where you want to trigger an HTTP POST, PUT, or DELETE request to your REST API integration, assign the app variable (e.g., CSRFToken from step 3) to the additional input parameter ( e.g csrfToken from step 4)

Details are shown in the screenshots below:

entry.title: SAP Loves ABAP (Again) — Insights from Constellation Research
entry.content:
At SAP TechEd 2024, SAP made a noteworthy announcement that has reignited interest in ABAP: support for ABAP has now been added to SAP Build. This addition brings together ABAP, pro-code (like Java), and low-code/no-code tools in one cohesive platform, empowering developers to modernize and build business applications with unparalleled flexibility.

Constellation Research recently published a research report, SAP Loves ABAP (Again), which dives into the impact of this development and highlights the ongoing significance of ABAP for SAP customers. The report traces ABAP's evolution from its roots in the mainframe days to the versatile, business-focused programming language it is today.

Constellation’s report explores how ABAP’s integration with SAP Build strengthens SAP’s tech stack by merging different coding paradigms into a single platform, ultimately allowing SAP customers to keep pace with changing business demands.

Check out Constellation Research's full report to understand how SAP is reaffirming its commitment to ABAP and what this means for developers, particularly in the SAP Build community!

entry.title: ASUG Tech Connect 2024 | Keynote Highlights & Resources
entry.content:
Today, the Americas’ SAP Users’ Group (ASUG) hosted the  first SAP TechEd on Tour stop in Florida! Together with Geoff Scott, CEO of ASUG, and Patrick Dineen, SVP Finance Transformation Systems at NBC Universal, we explored how SAP is helping organizations accelerate ERP modernization and drive innovation across industries with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), SAP Joule, SAP Build, and SAP Integration Suite. 

In this blog post, I've compiled all relevant links and demos presented during my segment of the keynote – and more!  

If you want to learn more about SAP TechEd on Tour and other stops, go to https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html.  

 

Best of TechEd – Virtual keynote recap  

The main SAP Business Technology Platform announcements from our virtual keynote focused on three key points: 

  • First: We help customers get faster, better, and automated: SAP BTP serves all their needs to innovate, integrate, and extend their systems.  
  • Second: We are enhancing the developer and user experience with our latest release of SAP Build, which now includes ABAP Cloud.   
  • And third: We are raising the bar for Business AI – for a whole new level of productivity.  

You will find all announcements shared at SAP TechEd 2024 in our News Guide and the replay of our opening keynote. 

 

Best of Platform 

SAP BTP is your platform of choice for application development, automation, integration, data & analytics, and Business AI. 

To support your journey with BTP, we provide a wide range of tools, best practices, resources, and reference architectures designed to help you:

  • Design and deploy enterprise-grade solutions,
  • accelerate time-to-value and optimize costs, and
  • foster collaboration and continuous learning in your architectural practices.

How to get started? At ASUG TechConnect, I highlighted some of the key tools and resources available to get you started effectively:

The SAP BTP Guidance Framework helps you design solutions that align with your company’s digital strategy. You can find more information here on SAP Help, and BTP reference architectures at the SAP Discovery Center. Adopt and adjust the architectures to your requirements by using the BTP solution diagram repository.

Or simply tune into our SAP TechEd session on demand:

  • Start your journey with SAP BTP and confidently accelerate adoption

The SAP Discovery Center provides you with hands-on guidance to implement BTP services  based on real use cases! You can activate them in your BTP landscape with the new quick account setup. With a single click you will get all services provisioned and deployed. Learn more about this in the respective SAP TechEd on-demand sessions:

  • Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center
  • Modernize your technology landscape with SAP BTP and SAP LeanIX solutions

The SAP Business Accelerator Hub equips you with additional building blocks to realize your digital strategy. More than 25,000 pre-built assets are already available, such as Integration flows, APIs, Business Processes, adapters, validated partner use cases and a lot more.

The SAP BTP GenAI starter kit empowers you to integrate AI capabilities into your infrastructure easily. Get a first overview in this blog post and more detailed information here on GitHub.

Once you’re set up with BTP, a world of possibilities opens up. In our keynote, I highlighted why BTP stands as the premier platform for embedding AI, accelerating developer productivity, building applications, and seamlessly integrating SAP with third-party solutions:

AI Foundation on BTP
The AI Foundation on BTP helps you make the most out of your SAP data and processes. You also get the broadest selection of foundational models from top providers—all in one place. Find out more through our SAP TechEd on-demand sessions:

  • Development with our generative AI hub, SDKs, and orchestration features
  • Enable more secure and responsible generative AI

Joule and Joule studio
By year-end, our AI copilot Joule will cover 80% of SAP's top workflows, enabling users to manage timesheets, view returns, create reports, and compare quotes through natural language. Integrated with SAP Build, it also boosts developer productivity. Discover additional insights in the following SAP TechEd on-demand session:

  • Extend Joule with SAP Build and develop custom enterprise skills

Joule Agents
Joule is going agentic! Powered by large language models, AI agents tackle complex business problems through planning, self-reflection, reasoning, and collaboration.

SAP Build x ABAP
Two years ago, we launched SAP Build as a unified low-code environment. Last year, we added SAP Build Code for AI-driven app development. Now, SAP Build also integrates directly with SAP S/4HANA, enabling BAPI and function module calls in the S/4HANA Private Cloud. And we now also included ABAP Cloud in our latest release of SAP Build!

We also introduced the new ABAP explain agent, allowing users to quickly understand complex ABAP code by generating explanations through large language models. Learn more in our TechEd on-demand sessions: 

  • Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build
  • Boost your coding efficiency: Explore Joule's ABAP developer capabilities

Integration Suite
With SAP Integration Suite, we offer you a modern solution to run your integration scenarios across SAP and third-party applications. You benefit from embedded AI, hundreds of connectors, and thousands of pre-built integrations. Already 75% of our customers use SAP Integration Suite to integrate with non-SAP systems. And we are introducing new integration adapters for Anaplan, Coupa, HubSpot, NetSuite ERP, Snowflake, and more.

  • Innovate now with a modern, AI-assisted integration suite

 

Best of Suite

With SAP BTP powering our suite of enterprise applications, we also explored how everything connects across our portfolio. A suite-first approach ensures a seamless, productive user experience throughout the workday.

As an example, we presented a demo that shows how the end users of our products can easily navigate across our solution portfolio via SAP Start. Infused by our copilot Joule, users can be more productive when setting up new teams, booking travel, or getting insights through SAP Analytics Cloud, for example. You can watch the full demo here.

  •  View our Integration Strategy Paper

 

Get started now!

To get started with BTP and Business AI, here are some of our missions in the SAP Discovery Center:

  • AI on BTP: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4338/4621/
  • GenAI with SAP Build Code: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4441/4727/
  • Gen AI with RAG for CAP: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4371/4655/ 

Here are some additional missions to kick off your journey with BTP: 

  • App Development on BTP: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4431/4717/
  • Integration Suite: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/3258/3327/
  • Best Of Suite: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4451/4737/

I hope you are all fired up now and ready to get your hands dirty.

We can’t wait to see what you will build!

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation 审批流介绍（基本审批人确立、串签会签等不同情况）
entry.content:
大家好！随着越来越多的用户使用SBPA（Build Process Automation）实现企业级自动化，构建审批流程也成为了常见的话题。在相关合作伙伴和客户的咨询下，针对串签、并签、会签等不同审批情况，我们也进行了一些内部实践，并在本文进行分享。

前提一： 已经完成SBPA服务订阅和环境部署，以及桌面Agent等相关代理配置。已有基础用户权限配置。

前提二： 我们将基于“批准并注册新供应商申请”的自动化流程和相关表单，针对不同审批流程做出加强和改变。(如果您已经对表单创建或工作流基本模块映射有所了解,也可以跳过以下步骤，直接前往文章末尾创建决策步骤）

 

前提二中的表单及相关工作流创建步骤：

创建新项目

从SAP Build Lobby进入，建立新项目：

等待系统创建新项目；创建完成后，会跳出disclaimer界面，选择接受，并进入新流程建立。

创建新流程

在新窗口中建立新流程及相关描述，选择create。

在此手册中我们需要建立一个基于供应商申请表单作为触发器的审批流，因此回到项目概览，继续进行表单创建。

创建申请表单

在新窗口中建立新流程及相关描述，选择create。

将新表单命名为“Vendor Application Form”；并点击 “创建”。

打开表单编辑器；点击“H1标题1”，输入“供应商申请表单”。

 

点击“文本”，并输入“供应商名称”。

 

点击“下拉列表”，并输入“供应商类型” 右侧选项改为 “1”, “2”

 

点击“文本”四次，并依序加入

采购组织

供应商联系方式

供应商联系地址

统一社会信用代码

 

点击 “保存”。

 

创建审批表单

在项目概览页中，点击“创建”，点击“审批”。

在名称中输入“Vendor Approval Form”，勾选“基于表单”，下拉选单中选择“Vendor Application Form”。点击“创建”。

    

在项目概览页中，点击“创建”，点击“表单”。

 

在名称中输入“BP Notification Form”，点击“创建”。

打开表单编辑器；点击“H1标题1”，输入“供应商创建成功”。

 

点击“段落”，输入“您申请的供应商已创建成功，请参考以下信息”。

 

加入两个“文本”，“供应商名称”与“供应商BP号”，并将两个文本设置为“只读”。

点击 “保存”。

在项目概览页中，点击“创建”，点击“数据类型”。

 

在创建数据类型的名称中输入“Approver”。

点击“新建字段”，在名称中输入“email”，类型选择“字符串”。点击“保存”。

 

再一次, 在项目概览页中，点击“创建”，点击“数据类型”。

在创建数据类型的名称中输入“Vendor”。

 

点击“新建字段”，依序在名称中输入

VendorName

Vendortype

PurcOrg

CreditCode

类型选择“字符串”。点击“保存”。

创建流程

在项目概览页中，点击“创建”，点击“过程”。

输入流程名称，“Vendor Creation”，并点击“创建”。

 

点击添加触发器>提交表单>Vendor Application Form。

添加审批>审批表单“Vendor Approve Form”。

 创建决策

在审批表单“Vendor Approve Form”之前加入一個決策, 來設置審批者

 

 

 

 

在名稱輸入Determine Approver, 点击创建

 

接着我们来决定审批人是谁，点击添加输入参数

名称、描述、类型都输入Vendor

 

点击添加输出参数，名称、描述、类型都输入Approver

点击Rules

 

规则名称与规则描述 输入Vendor Approver点击下一步

点击Vendor >PurcOrg> 下一步

点击Approver> email> 下一步

 

点击创建

 

在decision table决策表中写下具体规则

1. 单一审批人、单一条件

在PurcOrg下输入EXISTSIN[‘101’], email下输入 ‘您的登录邮箱’

点击保存，回到Vendor Creation流程

填写Decision Approver处理内容，点击输入，选择单一属性，并点击Vendor Application Form（触发器）下的统一社会信用代码，采购组织，供应商名称，供应商类型依序输入相对应的空格

 

点击Vendor Approval Form，"主题"拖拽与输入“请审批新的供应商+供应商名称”，“用户”中拖拽email

审批通过之后 在Approve下方加入Action(操作)以在S4HC系统中使用API创建供应商主数据。

（注：更多Action及测试部署的步骤在此文中将不会详细解释，我们重点汇会聚焦于审批的不同场景）

通过以上步骤，我们已经有了初步的表单、数据类型和审批流的场景，以下我们将介绍一些更复杂的场景（串签、并签等）

Pre-requisite

给子账户添加额外的用户（额外的审批人邮箱）

Go to Cockpit-Your Subaccount，选择Security-Users

 

将对应用户的BTP账户邮箱和user name输入（Trial中均为邮箱），并添加

 

给添加的用户assign SBPA对应的role collection:

 

创建新的申请表单和数据类型以适应复杂需求

新的供应商申请表需要加入“采购金额上限（万）”字段，且限定为数字类型：

 

添加Purcaselimit字段到Vendor数据类型，也限定为数字类型：

 

添加多个email字段到approver数据类型，文本类型：

 

在相关的输入中增加两者的映射：

在Decision中

 

在审批表中（在此之前请重新基于新的申请表生成审批表以引入新字段）

 

 

串签：部门主管+满足签核条件其他部门或主管审批（如采购金额limit）

 

决策表例子

任意供应商请求到达部门主管；

 

满足特定条件（采购金额在30-100万之间）到达其他部门主管审批；

 

Overview

 

 

审批流重新建立

 

新条件

 

 

期望场景

申请表填写，触发自动化

 

背景审查通过，到达第一位审批人员

 

第一位审批主管审批通过，到达第二位满足条件的审批主管：

 

两人均审批通过，系统自动在S4HC创建供应商主数据

 

 

并签：同时发出给所有人签，如两位或三位并签（其中一位核签通过则通过）

 

决策表例子

 

审批流重新建立

只需将审批表中的接收方用户输入两个审批方邮箱即可

 

 

期望场景

申请表填写，触发自动化

背景审查通过，到达所有审批人员，任意一人审批通过，开始创建客户主数据。

 

会签：需照会其他单位，此单位仅作comment，如财会单位提供意见，不用做审批

 

决策表例子

 

 

新的参考意见审批表和供应商审批表

参考意见审批表增加comment字段（非必填），以及中性按钮：

供应商审批表增加comment字段（只读），接下来会映射采用参考意见审批表的output(输出):

 

审批流重新建立

 

在新的审批表中增加字段映射

 

期望场景

申请表填写，触发自动化

背景审查通过，到达参考意见部门，提供对应参考意见（可选）， 进入下一步；

到达审批部门，批准对应供应商申请请求，系统主动在S4HC系统创建供应商主数据。

entry.title: Unlocking the Future: Why Upskilling with SAP Build Apps is Essential
entry.content:
In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, the demand for innovative and efficient business applications is higher than ever. With the ever-increasing need to streamline operations and enhance productivity, mastering modern app-building tools has become a critical skill for IT professionals and business leaders alike.

Why SAP Build Apps?
SAP Build Apps provide a robust, no-code platform that empowers developers and non-developers to create custom applications with ease. With intuitive drag-and-drop functionality, SAP Build Apps eliminates many of the complexities traditionally associated with app development, making it accessible for users of varying skill levels. By investing in SAP Build Apps, you're not just learning another tool—you're adopting a transformative skill that aligns with the future of business technology.

Learning Pathways to Success
To help you navigate your upskilling journey, the SAP Learning site with over 2 million learners offers free, self-paced learning journeys. These resources are designed to guide you from foundational knowledge to advanced competencies, ensuring you're fully equipped to build high-impact applications. Here are some must-explore Learning Journeys:

  • Compose and Automate with SAP Build: The No-Code Way: Perfect for those new to no-code app building, this journey introduces the core concepts and tools in SAP Build to help you quickly create and automate applications without complex coding.

  • Develop Apps with SAP Build Apps Using Drag-and-Drop Simplicity: This journey focuses on SAP Build’s drag-and-drop interface, teaching you how to design and deploy applications in a matter of hours. It's ideal for IT professionals looking to develop dynamic apps without diving deep into code.

  • Extending SAP S/4HANA with SAP Build Apps and Key User Extensibility: For those working with SAP S/4HANA, this path delves into extending existing SAP applications, enabling you to build custom solutions that seamlessly integrate with SAP’s ecosystem.

SAP Certification: Validating Your Skills
For backend developers seeking to bolster their credentials, the SAP Certified Associate - Backend Developer for SAP Cloud Programming Model certification provides an excellent opportunity to validate your expertise. This certification covers essential skills in the SAP Cloud environment, offering recognition and career advancement for those committed to mastering the backend of SAP technologies.

The Benefits of Investing in SAP Build Apps Skills
Investing your time and effort into learning SAP Build Apps is a strategic move that promises substantial returns. By mastering this powerful tool, you'll be well-equipped to meet the modern demands of app development, drive business success, and propel your career to new heights. Not only does SAP Build Apps provide you with a sought-after skill set, but it also enables you to build adaptable solutions that keep your business agile and future-ready.

Ready to unlock your potential? Visit the SAP Build Apps page on the SAP Learning site and start your journey towards success today.

entry.title: Access Control option for workspaces in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition
entry.content:
As organisations grow and diversify, so does the need for flexible, secure, and tailored access control within collaboration platforms.

SAP Build Work Zone, Advanced Edition addresses this need with its advanced workspace privacy and access options, tailored to meet a different range of business requirements. This post dives into how these features support unique access configurations, offering business leaders, employees, and external end users the control they need over collaborative workspaces.

Public vs. Private Workspaces: The Core Options

At the foundation of SAP Build Work Zone Advanced Edition are two main workspace types: Public and Private.

  • Public Workspaces: Ideal for general information accessible by all employees, such as HR policies, product updates, or company news. These spaces are searchable across the SAP Build Work Zone site, and ensure employees can easily locate essential resources.
  • Private Workspaces: Designed for limited access, these workspaces are only available to a selected group of members. Employees must be invited or added via a user list, with non-members unable to search or view the content, which helps ensure sensitive information stays within specified groups (e.g., finance teams, regional HR policies).

Worth mentioning, a user can also add private content in a public space, if he/she wants to share it with a smaller audience. This is done by selecting New Private Folder from the create content dropdown (as shown in the following screenshot)

 

External User Access

 In certain cases, companies may want to grant external users - such as suppliers, consultants, or partners - access to specific workspaces without exposing the entire SAP Build Work Zone site. By selecting an external access option (available for both Public and Private workspaces), workspace admins can securely share essential information or collaborate with users outside the organisation.

Workspaces with external access are marked as “External” to alert members to potential privacy considerations, helping them avoid sharing confidential information (see screenshot below).

Furthermore, as a workspace creator, you can also control whether other members can join the workspace and whether they can invite others without requiring your approval as the workspace admin. (See the screenshot below).

Customisable Collaboration Levels and Task Policy Management

Beyond access settings, SAP Build Work Zone provides collaboration level options tailored to the workspace's purpose and members’ needs:

  • Read-Only: Members can view content without contributing.-
  • Limited Collaboration: Members can interact with limited editing permissions.
  • Full Collaboration: Offers the highest level of engagement, allowing members to actively participate in workspace activities.

For workspaces with Limited or Full collaboration, Task Policy editing options further define control over task updates. Workspace administrators can decide if tasks should be editable by all members, limited to assignees and admins, or strictly restricted to creators and admins. This setup helps creating transparency and define task accountability according to the Workspace’s requirements (see screenshot below).

Upload and Approval Policies

Content-sharing policies are critical for managing sensitive information. Workspace admins have options to enforce upload and approval policies, which determine whether content (like PDFs, videos, or posts) can be shared freely by members or if admin review is required (see screenshot). This ensures that only authorised information appears in the workspace, particularly in cases where compliance or quality control is essential.

Non-Member Participation and Access Policies

Non-member participation provide additional layers of security and flexibility:

Collaboration Level: Admins can allow non-members to view content or restrict all collaborative tools and member details. For cases requiring transparency across a department or broader audience, enabling non-member access can facilitate information sharing without compromising security.

Access Policies: Some workspaces may require restricted access for specific employee subgroups (e.g., HR or Sales), instead of granting access to the full organisation. These access policies allow granular control of visibility and participation, ensuring the workspace meets unique business requirements.

These flexible customisations are designed to meet diverse organisational needs, whether that’s ensuring company-wide access to HR updates or creating secure, department-specific workspaces for internal projects or partnerships with external collaborators.

By leveraging these options, your organisation can optimise SAP Build Work Zone Advanced Edition to match your unique operational needs, balancing open collaboration with tailored, role-based access, thus protecting sensitive information and at the same time promoting effective teamwork.

SUMMARY

Decision Table: 

What I want to achieve?Which configurations I need:
A Workspace visible to all employees. Each employee could join and invite other members.

Create a Public Workspace + select checkbox:

  • Allow all members to invite users,
  • Allow users to join this workspace
A workspace visible to all employees, to but as a workspace admin I want to control who has access and I want to be the only one inviting members

Create a Public Workspace + leave unselected all other checkboxes.

A workspace which is visible to all employees AND external users (prior invitation)Create a Public Workspace + select checkbox:
  • External users can access this workspace and be invited as members
A workspace visible only to members and accessible via invitation. As a workspace admin, I want to be the only only one who can invite users (users cannot request to join).

Create a Private Workspace + leave unselected all other checkboxes.

A workspace visible only to members and accessible via invitation. Members can invite other users and users are able to request to join the workspace.

Create a Private Workspace + select checkbox:

  • Allow all members to invite users,
  • Allow users to request to join this workspace
A workspace which is visible to only to member BUT ALSO to some external users (prior invitation)Create a Private Workspace + select checkbox:
  • External users can access this workspace and be invited as members
A Workspace (private or public) to share information, in which members can only view and download resources.After creating the Workspace, click next and Select collaboration Level Read Only.

A Workspace (private or public)  in which members can collaborate, and take some actions on existing content such as view, comment, download. They can also handle tasks but not create ones.

After creating the Workspace, click next and Select collaboration Level Limited.

Remember to select task policy according to needs:

  • Tasks are editable by creators and workspace admins. Status can be updated by task assignee.
  • Tasks are editable by assignees, creators, and workspace admins. Status can be updated by all members.
  • Task details and status can be updated by all members.

A Workspace (private or public)  in which members can collaborate, and take all actions on content such as view, comment, download, edit, rename and delete. They can also create and handle tasks.

All members can upload content.

After creating the Workspace, click next and Select collaboration Level Full + select checkbox Upload Policy.

A Workspace (private or public)  in which members can collaborate, and take all actions on content such as view, comment, download, edit, rename and delete. They can also create and handle tasks.

All members can upload content, but as an admin you want to approve content sharing.

After creating the Workspace, click next and Select collaboration Level Full + select checkbox Upload Policy and select checkbox Approval Policy.

In a public or Private space, you want to share some content visible only to a subset of specific users.

Click on the hamburger menu on your Workpage, select Content from the available options, click on the Create dropdown menu and select New Private Folder.

entry.title: The Path Forward: SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA Are Retired: Here’s Your Next Step with SAP Build Process Automation 

As part of SAP's continuous innovation, SAP Build Process Automation has been introduced as the successor to SAP Workflow Management within the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). With the recent announcement of the retirement of SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA, now is the time for customers to plan their transition to SAP Build Process Automation. This next-generation tool is designed to bring powerful, unified capabilities for process automation, enabling you to build workflows, deploy automations, and enhance productivity across your organization.

Why Transition to SAP Build Process Automation?

With the retirement of SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA, SAP Build Process Automation has emerged as the comprehensive tool for creating intelligent workflows and automations. Designed to bring together workflow, RPA, and AI-driven process automation, SAP Build Process Automation enables business and IT teams to work collaboratively to simplify complex, repetitive tasks and drive efficiency like never before. 

If you're currently using SAP Workflow Management or Intelligent RPA, now is the time to explore how SAP Build Process Automation can empower your teams with its advanced capabilities. This transition also ensures ongoing support, access to the latest features, and alignment with SAP’s innovation roadmap. 

Join Our Exclusive Migration Webinars 

To help you make this transition seamless, we’re hosting a series of exclusive webinars. Our product management team will provide guidance on migrating to SAP Build Process Automation and answer any questions. You’ll gain insights into: 

  • Migration Strategies: How to move from SAP Workflow Management and Intelligent RPA to SAP Build Process Automation smoothly. 
  • Advanced Features: Explore new capabilities for workflow, RPA, and AI within the SAP Build environment. 
  • Best Practices: Get practical tips and resources to accelerate your migration journey. 

Mark your calendar and choose the session that fits your needs: 

SAP Workflow Management Migration Webinar                             SAP Intelligent RPA Migration Webinar 

📅Date: Tuesday, December 3, 2024                                                     📅  Date: Thursday, December 5, 2024 

⏰Time: 10 am EST                                                                                ⏰Time: 10 am EST   

[Register Here]                                                                                         [Register Here] 

 

Additional Resources 

To further support your migration journey, we’ve curated helpful resources: 

  • SAP Workflow Management - The Path forward
  • Transition to SAP Build Process Automation  
  • Blog post on SAP Community  

Don’t miss this opportunity to learn how SAP Build Process Automation is transforming the automation landscape. Whether you’re in business or IT, these webinars are designed to give you the insights and support you need to make a successful transition. 

Let’s make automation simpler, smarter, and more powerful with SAP Build Process Automation. Register today to unlock the full potential of SAP Build for your organization!
